props.name: setBlocksRaycasts
props.tooltip: If set, the raycast blocking of the alpha target is switched off while the alpha value is (near) zero, and on otherwise. This prevents a fully faded-out element (e.g. a blocker) from still catching input. Toggles Graphic.raycastTarget and/or CanvasGroup.blocksRaycasts. Only relevant for the alpha animation.

props.name: duration
props.tooltip: Duration of this animation, excluding delay.

props.name: delay
props.tooltip: Delay for the beginning of the animation (when played forwards)

props.name: animationSpeed
props.tooltip: Animation speed

props.name: setSpeedForSlaves
props.tooltip: Propagates the speed setting to all slaves (hierarchically)

props.name: backwardsPlayable
props.tooltip: If set to false, this animation is skipped when playing backwards

props.name: backwardsAnimation
props.tooltip: If set, this animation is played instead when playing backwards

props.name: gotoStartOnBackwards
props.tooltip: If set to true, the animation instantly goes to start when played backwards

props.name: autoStart
props.tooltip: Automatically start the animation as soon as it becomes visible

props.name: autoOnEnable
props.tooltip: Automatically start the animation every time it becomes visible

props.name: setOnStart
props.tooltip: Set the animation beginning values as it becomes visible, but don't start it

props.name: numberOfLoops
props.tooltip: Number of loops. -1: infinite loops 0: no loops, >0: Arbitrary number of loops

props.name: finishInstantOnResolutionChange
props.tooltip: Finish animation instantly on screen resolution change. This is important to set for animations, which differ in landscape and portrait.

props.name: slaveAnimations
props.tooltip: Slave animations which are automatically started when this animation is started.

props.name: timingWorkaroundActive
props.tooltip: Currently, the calculation of the backwards time is broken. It's pretty complicated and I don't have the time to fix it. If you do: You know where the Repo is :) Activating this workaround sets the backward delay to 0, and lets you enter a correction factor for the backwards time.

props.name: setBlocksRaycasts
props.tooltip: If set, the raycast blocking of the alpha target is switched off while the alpha value is (near) zero, and on otherwise. This prevents a fully faded-out element (e.g. a blocker) from still catching input. Toggles Graphic.raycastTarget and/or CanvasGroup.blocksRaycasts. Only relevant for the alpha animation.

props.name: baseDelayPerChild
props.tooltip: A delay, which is added to every child

props.name: delayPerChild
props.tooltip: An increasing delay, which is added to every child. Negative values reverse the direction of children.

props.name: baseDurationPerChild
props.tooltip: Base Duration per child. If left 0, each child sets its duration itself

props.name: durationPerChild
props.tooltip: Duration per child. If left 0, each child sets its duration itself. Negative values reverse the direction of children.

props.name: segments
props.tooltip: Number of segments approximating the circle. Higher = rounder, but more triangles. At low values (4, 5, 6, 8) you effectively get a regular polygon.

props.name: frameSize
props.tooltip: Frame size. When set to 0, the image is completely filled. To work properly, this should always be less than the shape's primary radius/size when used with frame.

props.name: fadeSize
props.tooltip: Fades out the edges of the image. Very useful for antialiasing, but can also be used for other purposes (e.g. soft shadow)

props.name: fadeColor
props.tooltip: The Transparent color

props.name: invertMask
props.tooltip: Invert stencil mask. Useful for cutouts.

props.name: usePadding
props.tooltip: Use padding. This is most useful for adjusting stretchable masks.

props.name: padding
props.tooltip: Padding

props.name: useFixedSize
props.tooltip: Assign a fixed size instead of using rect transform boundaries. The fixed size is centered to the pivot. This is most useful for adjusting stretchable masks. X and y are offsets.

props.name: fixedSize
props.tooltip: Fixed size

props.name: gradientSimple
props.tooltip: Ui Simple Gradient component. Mandatory if you want to use the 'SimpleGradientColors' getters+setters.

props.name: uniformSizeOffset
props.tooltip: If true, a single size offset value is applied to both width and height. If false, X and Y are independent.

props.name: sizeOffset
props.tooltip: Offset added to the rect's width / height, applied symmetrically around the rect center. Positive values grow the shape, negative values shrink it. When m_uniformSizeOffset is true, only the X component is used for both axes.

props.name: gradientSimple
props.tooltip: Simple Gradient. Mandatory if you want to use the 'SimpleGradientColors' getters+setters.

props.name: cornerSegments
props.tooltip: Corner segments. The more, the rounder. But keep an eye on performance; more corner segments mean more triangles and longer creation time. Between 5 and 10 should be sufficient for most tasks.

props.name: radius
props.tooltip: Corner radius. To work properly, this should always be greater than frame size (when used with frame)

props.name: frameSize
props.tooltip: Frame size. When set to 0, the image is completely filled. To work properly, this should always be less than the shape's primary radius/size when used with frame.

props.name: fadeSize
props.tooltip: Fades out the edges of the image. Very useful for antialiasing, but can also be used for other purposes (e.g. soft shadow)

props.name: fadeColor
props.tooltip: The Transparent color

props.name: invertMask
props.tooltip: Invert stencil mask. Useful for cutouts.

props.name: usePadding
props.tooltip: Use padding. This is most useful for adjusting stretchable masks.

props.name: padding
props.tooltip: Padding

props.name: useFixedSize
props.tooltip: Assign a fixed size instead of using rect transform boundaries. The fixed size is centered to the pivot. This is most useful for adjusting stretchable masks. X and y are offsets.

props.name: fixedSize
props.tooltip: Fixed size

props.name: gradientSimple
props.tooltip: Ui Simple Gradient component. Mandatory if you want to use the 'SimpleGradientColors' getters+setters.

props.name: uniformSizeOffset
props.tooltip: If true, a single size offset value is applied to both width and height. If false, X and Y are independent.

props.name: sizeOffset
props.tooltip: Offset added to the rect's width / height, applied symmetrically around the rect center. Positive values grow the shape, negative values shrink it. When m_uniformSizeOffset is true, only the X component is used for both axes.

props.name: spikeCount
props.tooltip: Number of star spikes.

props.name: innerRadiusRatio
props.tooltip: Inner radius as a fraction of the outer radius (0 = notches at center, 1 = no notch). Typical pointy stars: 0.4-0.55. Sunburst / shallow notches: 0.85-0.95.

props.name: rotation
props.tooltip: Rotation around the rect center, in degrees. 0 = first spike points straight up.

props.name: insetStrategy
props.tooltip: How the inner perimeter is computed when a frame or fade is active.

props.name: miterLimit
props.tooltip: Miter limit (only used by InsetStrategy.Miter). Maximum ratio of miter length to inset; at sharper tips, the miter is clamped along the bisector. Higher = sharper tips preserved, lower = tips blunted earlier.

props.name: frameSize
props.tooltip: Frame size. When set to 0, the image is completely filled. To work properly, this should always be less than the shape's primary radius/size when used with frame.

props.name: fadeSize
props.tooltip: Fades out the edges of the image. Very useful for antialiasing, but can also be used for other purposes (e.g. soft shadow)

props.name: fadeColor
props.tooltip: The Transparent color

props.name: invertMask
props.tooltip: Invert stencil mask. Useful for cutouts.

props.name: usePadding
props.tooltip: Use padding. This is most useful for adjusting stretchable masks.

props.name: popupMenuPrefab
props.tooltip: Prefab for the popup menu shown when the dropdown opens.

props.name: selectedLabel
props.tooltip: Label that shows the currently selected item. Optional.

props.name: statusAnimation
props.tooltip: Optional animation played forwards when the dropdown opens and backwards when it closes.

props.name: hoverAnimation
props.tooltip: Optional hover animation. Paused while the popup is open so the UiPopup ClickCatcher cannot fire pointer-exit events that would reset the hover state.

props.name: maxPopupHeight
props.tooltip: Maximum popup height in canvas pixels. If the content is taller a vertical scrollbar appears. Values <= 0 use the popup prefab's own default.

props.name: offset
props.tooltip: Fine-positioning offset applied to the popup relative to the anchor (canvas pixels).

props.name: stringItemPrefab
props.tooltip: Optional prefab used for string items in the popup. Must contain a TMP_Text component. When null, items are created procedurally.

props.name: selectedItemColor
props.tooltip: Text color applied to the selected item in the popup.

props.name: deselectedItemColor
props.tooltip: Text color applied to unselected items in the popup.

props.name: autoLoadResources
props.tooltip: If enabled, this panel automatically loads its declared resources during Awake(). Use only for panels with static resources. Do NOT enable for dialogs or panels that compute resources dynamically at runtime.

props.name: staticAmplitude
props.tooltip: Time-independent UV offset amplitude. Always applied, no per-frame cost. Use this for a baseline distortion that breaks tiling even when animation is off.

props.name: staticFrequency
props.tooltip: Waves per UV unit for the static layer, separately for X and Y. The wobble in X depends on vertex.uv.y and vice versa, so the deformation is perpendicular to the frequency axis. Typical baseline: 2..4 (broad undulations).

props.name: animate
props.tooltip: Master switch for the animated layer. When off, the modifier is fully passive — no per-frame SetDirty, only the static distortion is in the mesh.

props.name: dynamicAmplitude
props.tooltip: Amplitude of the animated UV offset, added on top of the static one. Set to 0 to keep animation enabled but disable the dynamic component.

props.name: dynamicFrequency
props.tooltip: Waves per UV unit for the dynamic layer. Same rule as static frequency. Pair with a different static frequency for coarse-baseline + fine-ripple (or vice versa).

props.name: speed
props.tooltip: Time multiplier for the dynamic layer. 0 = animation enabled but frozen, 0.5 = slow flow, 2+ = frantic.

props.name: useUnscaledTime
props.tooltip: When on, dynamic layer uses Time.unscaledTime (continues during pause / time-scale changes).

props.name: layer
props.tooltip: The layer in which the view is shown.

props.name: isFullScreen
props.tooltip: When full screen, all other layers below are hidden.

props.name: hideOnOccludedByFullScreen
props.tooltip: Dialog is hidden when occluded by a full screen dialog.

props.name: hideOnOccludedByFullScreenInstant
props.tooltip: Dialog is hidden instantly when occluded by a full screen dialog.

props.name: useGlobalCanvasScalerTemplate
props.tooltip: Uses the global canvas scaler template in Ui toolkit configuration (if it is set).

props.name: autoDestroyOnHide
props.tooltip: When the view is closed via Hide(), the view is automatically destroyed or pooled.

props.name: poolable
props.tooltip: When checked, the dialog is pooled instead of destroyed.

props.name: closeButtons
props.tooltip: Most UiViews need one or more close buttons. You can optionally define those here.

props.name: backgroundMusicId
props.tooltip: Optional background music track id (a MusicTrack Id from the UiSoundConfig). When this view is shown, UiMusic crossfades to that track. Leave empty to leave the current music unchanged.

props.tooltip: Dialog is hidden when occluded by a full screen dialog.

props.name: hideOnOccludedByFullScreenInstant
props.tooltip: Dialog is hidden instantly when occluded by a full screen dialog.

props.name: useGlobalCanvasScalerTemplate
props.tooltip: Uses the global canvas scaler template in Ui toolkit configuration (if it is set).

props.name: autoDestroyOnHide
props.tooltip: When the view is closed via Hide(), the view is automatically destroyed or pooled.

props.name: poolable
props.tooltip: When checked, the dialog is pooled instead of destroyed.

props.name: closeButtons
props.tooltip: Most UiViews need one or more close buttons. You can optionally define those here.

props.name: backgroundMusicId
props.tooltip: Optional background music track id (a MusicTrack Id from the UiSoundConfig). When this view is shown, UiMusic crossfades to that track. Leave empty to leave the current music unchanged.

props.name: autoLoadResources
props.tooltip: If enabled, this panel automatically loads its declared resources during Awake(). Use only for panels with static resources. Do NOT enable for dialogs or panels that compute resources dynamically at runtime.

props.name: closeButton
props.tooltip: Close button. Optional.

props.name: tabContentContainer
props.tooltip: All Tabs are placed in this container. The container should contain a matching layout group to organize the tabs.

props.name: pageContentContainer
props.tooltip: All Pages are placed in this container. Pages should be set to match this container (e.g. stretching)

props.name: tabInfos
props.tooltip: Tab Infos. Note that if you enter prefabs here and don't instantiate them yourself by code, you need to check 'Instantiate Tab Infos On Start'

props.name: instantiateTabInfosOnStart
props.tooltip: Check this if you are using prefabs in your 'Tab Infos' and don't instantiate them yourself by code.

props.name: tabsParent
props.tooltip: Tabs parent object. Used to switch tab display on and off (a tab dialog with only one page doesn't make sense)

props.name: autoHideTabIfOnlyOne
props.tooltip: If this is checked, the 'Tabs Parent' object is hidden, if there is only one tab

props.name: popupContainer
props.tooltip: The panel that is repositioned near the anchor element.

props.name: contentContainer
props.tooltip: The scroll content parent. Items are instantiated here.

props.name: scrollRect
props.tooltip: ScrollRect used to scroll the item list. When assigned, popup height is computed automatically and capped at Max Popup Height.

props.name: maxPopupHeight
props.tooltip: Maximum popup height in canvas pixels. If the content is taller, a vertical scrollbar appears. Can be overridden per-call via Options.MaxHeight.

props.name: prefilledItemPrefabs
props.tooltip: Item prefabs instantiated into the menu when it opens (inspector pre-fill). Items are instantiated in array order before any code-provided items.

props.name: clickCatcher
props.tooltip: CanvasGroup of the full-screen click-catcher child (placed below the overlay panels). Blocking is engaged for the whole run and normalized off on awake — the serialized state is irrelevant.

props.name: overlayTimeoutSeconds
props.tooltip: Failsafe (unscaled seconds): longest wait for a panel's EvOnEndHide before logging an error and advancing. Guards a panel that never reports done. Must exceed the longest plausible user dwell (reading a changelog). <= 0 waits indefinitely.

props.name: enableLogging
props.tooltip: Verbose UiLog output for each show / advance / completion / state change.

props.name: autoLoadResources
props.tooltip: If enabled, this panel automatically loads its declared resources during Awake(). Use only for panels with static resources. Do NOT enable for dialogs or panels that compute resources dynamically at runtime.

props.name: state
props.tooltip: Desired state of the targets at runtime.

props.name: target
props.tooltip:
Children: all direct children of this GameObject.
List: only the GameObjects listed below.

props.name: objects
props.tooltip: Targets to apply the state to. Used only when Target is List.

props.name: doCloneMaterial
props.tooltip: If checked, a clone of the original material is created. Usually you want to enable this, because otherwise all other assets which use the material are affected.

props.name: additionalMouseOver
props.tooltip: Additional mouse over graphic (optional)

props.name: additionalMouseOverDuration
props.tooltip: Mouse over fade duration (optional)

props.name: ensureChildVisibilityDuration
props.tooltip: Duration of tween to make child visible. 0 means instant.

props.name: ensureChildVisibilityPadding
props.tooltip: Padding (Fraction of child width). If a child isn't the first or the last child, it makes sense to also display the neighbor tabs. This distance is used for that.

props.name: innerScrollRect
props.tooltip: The inner ScrollRect (primary). Required.

props.name: outerScrollRect
props.tooltip: The outer ScrollRect (secondary). Usually a parent. Will be auto-resolved if null.

props.name: optionalStyleConfig
props.tooltip: Set a specific UiStyleConfig. If left 'none', the global UiMainStyleConfig is used. Note that ALL appliers need to also have set the same config!

props.name: applierListFixed
props.tooltip: Check this if you want a fixed list of style appliers to be handled. If unchecked, all objects in the child hierarchy are handled

props.name: handleDynamicallyAddedAppliers
props.tooltip: Check this if you want dynamically added and removed style appliers to be handled

props.name: mode
props.tooltip: How this control's automatic UI sound is overridden.

props.name: redirectType
props.tooltip: Sound type to play instead of the detected one (used when Mode is Redirect).

props.name: customSound
props.tooltip: Custom sound to play, bypassing the shared config (used when Mode is Custom).
